extraction.one_line_summary: KRISTOFF Alexander won the bunch sprint on final Matrah Corniche lap after CAVAGNA Rémi's solo breakaway was caught on the final climb.
tactical_patterns: CAVAGNA Rémi went solo from five-man break on Matrah Corniche climb, forcing peloton to see him both sides on laps.
HAAS Nathan and Katusha attacked final climb to exploit peloton's breakaway-chasing fatigue rather than wait for leadout.
Trek-Segafredo controlled final lap for NIZZOLO Giacomo despite 32-second deficit, revealing sprint team trumps GC protection on ceremonial stage.

rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: positive: Attacked solo from breakaway on climb with technical risk-taking, forcing extended peloton chase despite inevitable catch.
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: positive: Won sprint on Matrah Corniche after week-long drought, suggesting form peaked late rather than faded early.
rider_signals.HAAS Nathan: neutral: Attacked final climb with Katusha despite not being in sprint contention, suggesting opportunistic GC time bonus hunt.
rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexey: positive: Won GC by 11 seconds over LÓPEZ, defending slim margin on technical circuit stage.
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: negative: Trek-Segafredo committed full leadout but failed to deliver stage win despite controlling final lap.
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: neutral: Challenged KRISTOFF in sprint finish, confirming readiness for reduced bunch gallop over pure field sprint.
